reviews.review_msg: Chill guy. My bigger problem is with the TAs. Their feedback is nonsensical and they take about a business month to respond to emails. A lot of the time it feels like instructions are unclear. TAs are the worst part of this course no doubt to me. A lot of the reading isn't actually needed to suceed but is interesting. BAD TAs.

Dr. Eigenfeldt is super nice, he was always accessible and took the time to explain things for this online course. The course is tough, you need an above and beyond understanding to get an A on assignments, even with great feedback, it's hard to improve, it was an interesting course but if you want an easy A upper div elective, this isn't the one!
Next to no criteria is given for assignments, and the TAs take forever to respond to emails when you reach out asking about criteria. Professor is also a prolific AI user

Straightforward course and GPA booster. Group assignments are a little annoying, but they take at most 15-20 minutes to do, keep up with the weekly individual assignments, do most of the quizzes and put in effort into the essays, and you'll pass easily. He's also really quick to respond if you need help.

Very boring and redundant assignments.The 1st one was fine but then the 2nd and 3rd assignments come around and they are the exact same thing and getting through them becomes so dreadful. I am honestly so disappointed by this course not only did I not learn anything but it is more of a gpa tanker than a booster. Please take another elective instead

Don't take this class unless the structure changes. MC quizzes are extremely subjective which makes it difficult to do well IDK why he would make the quizzes like that since film is up to the audience's interpretation there is no "wrong" interpretation if it can be justified with course concepts but you cant do that in a MC format Not a GPA BOOSTER

Arne seems like a decent guy but his course CA386 is just atrocious. He is hyper critical about the most superfluous details in assignments, gives the most nonsensical and subjective quizzes/tests and even admits to designing them poorly in emails. The EXTENSIVE readings literally do not help at all, I have lost my will to even bother trying. AVOID

CA 386: content of this class is easy, workload is ridiculous. The quizzes are fine, multiple choice, kinda subjective. THE ASSIGNMENTS ARE AWFUL! 3 total assignments, asking for the longest, most tedious process about old, uninteresting movies. He asks for a crazy amount of work (like 15 pages) when so unnecessary to show ur understanding.

The prof made a mistake and deleted the original discussion groups, thus "losing" my discussion assignment, then dared to mark it as "incomplete". I tried contacting the prof and TA about this, and neither has responded. My work is still "lost" and "incomplete" even though I submitted it. This class is so messy; if you can, avoid this course.
trash course and a doodoo prof. Absolute waste of time. Couldn't be bothered with this course if it weren't for my B-Hum requirements. Terrible experience

Took this course as an elective and GPA booster. The TAs do mark tough but if you put in the work you'll at least get A-. Currently on a co-op and this course was pretty light, as in nothing to study for. But very writing intensive, so be sure to brush up on your writing skills and pay attention to course terms. Grading is very vague though...
Worst class I've taken in a while

Grading is tough depending on the ta. This class took more of my time than my other main classes. Out of the 5 assignments so far only 2 have been marked. The readings are mainly random history facts and less about how to analyze music.
The worst class I've ever taken.

Honestly avoid this class only if u have this prof. There is no feedback given and when you question a grade, in return, you get an egoistical response. The material has a vague description but marks are cut off without reason. Comments are not helpful and do not indicate what was amiss. Overall, the most disrespectful and rude prof I've ever had.

This course should be a writing course. Expect ~7 assignments/exams with ~800 words. Everyone I know received the same 3 marks (B/B+/A-) for every assignment, so it may be impossible to get higher or lower. TAs got my name wrong and seem to not care. LOTS of reading. but is usually interesting. But Arne is hilarious: Genshin music was on our final.

Material is all taught through Canvas readings

Quizzes are short/easy, all with 2 attempts

Assignments are all written with modestly long word counts

Finals the same as assignment

This class was fun but I only recommend it if you truly appreciate music across many genres, its applications in media, as well as the contexts they are performed in.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. Why did he think assigning NUMEROUS group projects for an ONLINE class is a good idea? The assignments have very vague instructions, yet are graded very harshly. TA feedback would say you did a great job but proceed to give a B-. Certain criteria like "creativity" on a formal analysis paper make no sense.
Incredibly callous and ableist, typical music prof.

This should be an easy online course, but the delivery (reading walls of text on canvas) was extremely boring. Content itself was not difficult to understand, but assignments were returned late or not at all (even after the strike was over), and there was NO feedback or rubric to see why marks were taken off. Overall frustrating and a waste of time
